questions.q: The amount set aside out of profits every year before the appropriation of profit to provide for future expenditure expected to arise on account of depreciation of assets is called:
A company purchased a machine for ₹ 50,000. It has a useful life of 5 years and scrap value of ₹ 5,000. The rate of depreciation is:
The difference between the cost of asset and its residual value is known as:
A company makes a provision for bad debts as 5% of its turnover. Its turnover for the year is ₹ 1,000,000. What is the amount of provision to be made?
The accounting treatment of provision is different from that of reserves. Explain.
questions.answer: A provision is a liability whereas reserve is an equity.

questions.q: The amount transferred to general reserve is:
A provision for depreciation is created at the beginning of the year and is ₹ 10,000. During the year, an asset of ₹ 20,000 is sold. Prepare the statement of changes in provisions.
A company has provision for depreciation of ₹ 1,00,000. It writes off ₹ 20,000 of this provision. Prepare the journal entry for the same.
What do you mean by accumulated profit?
questions.answer: The profit earned by an enterprise from its inception up to the date of balance sheet.

questions.q: A company has a provision for doubtful debts of ₹ 20,000. It writes off ₹ 5,000 of this provision. Prepare the journal entry for the same.
questions.answer: Bad Debts Recovered ₹ 5,000, Provision for Doubtful Debts ₹ 5,000
